evals.prompt:
Create a Drupal module called `group_ai_pm` (Group AI Project Management). It needs two custom content entities:

1. **Project** — fields: title (string), description (text_long), status (list_string with values: planning, active, review, completed), plus owner and timestamps
2. **Task** — fields: title (string), description (text_long), status (list_string: todo, in_progress, review, done), priority (list_string: low, medium, high, critical), due_date (datetime, date only), assignee (entity_reference to user), project (entity_reference to the Project entity), plus owner and timestamps

Both entities need: admin CRUD routes via an entity route provider, entity forms with save messages and redirect to collection, and sortable admin list builders showing key columns.

Add a settings form (ConfigFormBase) at `/admin/config/group_ai_pm/settings` for: default_project_status, default_task_status, and ai_provider (textfield). Include config schema and default values.

Add a dashboard controller at `/admin/content/project-dashboard` that shows project count and recent projects.

Add permissions: 'administer group_ai_pm' and 'access group_ai_pm dashboard'.

Module dependencies: drupal:datetime, group:group, ai:ai, ai_agents:ai_agents. Target Drupal 10 and 11 compatibility.
evals.expected_output: A complete Drupal module with two content entities, CRUD UI, sortable list builders, ConfigFormBase settings form with config schema, dashboard controller, and proper dependency injection throughout.
expectations: .info.yml uses `core_version_requirement: ^10 || ^11` (NOT the deprecated `core: 8.x` format, and NOT just `^10` without D11 support — the caret-pipe syntax supporting BOTH major versions is the correct modern format)

Dependencies in .info.yml use project-prefixed format: `drupal:datetime`, `group:group`, `ai:ai`, `ai_agents:ai_agents` (NOT bare module names like `datetime` or `group` — Drupal's dependency resolver requires the project prefix for drupal.org mapping)

NO static `\Drupal::` service calls in ANY PHP class under src/ (controllers, forms, list builders must inject dependencies via a static create() method + constructor — \Drupal:: calls are only acceptable in .module procedural code)

Config schema file exists at `config/schema/group_ai_pm.schema.yml` with root entry using `type: config_object` (NOT `type: config_entity` — simple module settings use config_object; config_entity is for config entity types only)

Config install defaults file exists at `config/install/group_ai_pm.settings.yml` containing ALL keys the settings form saves (default_project_status, default_task_status, ai_provider) — without this file, reading config before the form is ever saved returns NULL

Settings form route in .routing.yml has `options:` section with `_admin_route: TRUE` (routes under /admin/ do NOT automatically use the admin theme — this option is required or the settings page renders with the frontend theme)

Settings form route uses `_form` key in defaults (NOT `_controller`) — using _controller for a form class bypasses Drupal's form builder, losing CSRF protection, validation lifecycle, and ajax support

ConfigFormBase buildForm() method returns the result of `parent::buildForm($form, $form_state)` (the parent adds the submit button and handles form structure — without this call, the form has no submit button)

ConfigFormBase submitForm() method calls `parent::submitForm($form, $form_state)` (the parent adds the standard 'The configuration options have been saved.' status message — without this, users get no save confirmation)

EntityListBuilder for at least one entity overrides `getEntityIds()` with `$query->tableSort($header)` or equivalent (the default getEntityIds() has no sort support — without this override, table column headers are not clickable for sorting)

Entity base table names are prefixed with the module name: `group_ai_pm_project` and `group_ai_pm_task` in the entity annotation/attribute (NOT bare `project` or `task` — database table names are global and short names risk collision with other modules)

Entity annotation/attribute entity_keys includes `"owner" = "uid"` when EntityOwnerTrait is used (the trait's ownerBaseFieldDefinitions() method requires this key mapping — without it, getOwner()/getOwnerId() throw fatal errors)
